text: Six months post-colectomy, the surgeon performs a circumferential incision around the stoma site in the left lower quadrant. The loop ileostomy is released, temporarily sealed with a trio-viral stick, and returned to the abdomen.

The surgeon achieves pneumoperitoneum using a single port access device. The inferior mesenteric artery is ligated at the superior artery level. Dissection is performed through the avascular plane posterior to the mesorectum, extending down to the levator muscles. Once the levator muscles are reached, dissection continues bilaterally. Anterior mobilization is performed, followed by a digital rectal exam to confirm the dissection level. The laparoscopic GIA-XVI stapler is fired twice to complete the procedure.

A 15 to 20 centimeter ileal pouch is constructed using ILA-100 and TA-13 staplers. A leak test is performed on the pouch, showing no leakage. An EEA-XXVIII anvil is inserted into the pouch, and under laparoscopic view, an intracorporeal anastomosis is created. A poshoscopy is performed to inspect the anastomosis.
